perTicker.profileNotes: Main issue: signal fires during secular corrections (NVDA -50%, MSFT -30%). Golden cross gate is the #1 fix. Also try: require MACD histogram recovering (higher low) as additional gate.
Already strong performer (LLY 82%, ABBV 94%). Focus on maintaining performance and refining exit timing. Earnings surprises are the main risk.

NEE (53.8%) and DUK (57.1%) are borderline. PCG (73.8%) and AEP (78.9%) are strong. Rate sensitivity is the key driver. When 10Y yield is falling (TLT rising), utilities buy signals are much more reliable. Add TLT confirmation gate.
Main issue: signal fires during secular corrections (NVDA -50%, MSFT -30%). Golden cross gate is the #1 fix. Also try: require MACD histogram recovering (higher low) as additional gate.

PLD (3.8% WR) and WELL (0 trades) are broken. Root cause: 200SMA slope threshold too high for rate-beaten REITs. Fix: (1) lower slopeThreshold to 0.003, (2) add TLT gate (buy only when TLT 20SMA > TLT 50SMA), (3) consider dividend yield support as additional buy signal.

TSLA is the most volatile (50% swings). HD (45% win rate) and NKE (33% win rate) are structural losers in current downturn. Separate TSLA/AMZN (growth) from HD/MCD/NKE (value) with different thresholds.

NEM (36.4% WR) is the worst — gold miners are anti-correlated with dollar. FCX (42%) is copper-proxy, very cyclical. LIN (26.3%) is counter-intuitive: defensive chemical company but algorithm signals poorly. Fix: NEM needs dollar-index gate (buy NEM only when DXY falling).
Main issue: signal fires during secular corrections (NVDA -50%, MSFT -30%). Golden cross gate is the #1 fix. Also try: require MACD histogram recovering (higher low) as additional gate.

BAC and banks are rate-cycle sensitive (12.5% win rate). Key improvement: add yield curve slope proxy (10Y-2Y). BAC signals in rate-inversion environment should be suppressed. JPM/V/MA are more resilient.

BAC and banks are rate-cycle sensitive (12.5% win rate). Key improvement: add yield curve slope proxy (10Y-2Y). BAC signals in rate-inversion environment should be suppressed. JPM/V/MA are more resilient.
No profile defined for this ticker.

GE (100% WR) and CAT (87.5%) are stars. UNP (10% WR) and HON (43% WR) are structural underperformers. UNP: slope gate misfires during supply chain disruption years. RTX (50%): binary defense spending cycles.

CVX (80%) and COP (71%) work well. XOM (69%) solid. EOG (70%) good. SLB (67%) good. Energy works when oil price is rising. Key: add oil price proxy (USO momentum) as confirmation. SLB most service-company like — needs GEX-of-oil filter.

Strong across board (PEP 80%, PG 71%, KO 64%). Main issue is KO — too many signals (36 buys) vs COST (25 buys). ATR% < 1 is an issue for slow-moving staples. Consider lowering ATR% bullish threshold from 2.0% to 1.5%.

CVX (80%) and COP (71%) work well. XOM (69%) solid. EOG (70%) good. SLB (67%) good. Energy works when oil price is rising. Key: add oil price proxy (USO momentum) as confirmation. SLB most service-company like — needs GEX-of-oil filter.

Bimodal sector: META (100% WR) and GOOGL (37.5% WR) are very different. Consider sub-profiling. DIS (-49% BnH) and T (+22% BnH) require value vs trend differentiation. NFLX (26.3%) suffers binary earnings moves.
